name: Chicken with Buttermilk Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix the chicken thighs with 1/2 cup buttermilk.
2. On waxed paper, mix together chopped fresh thyme, salt, and 1/2 cup flour to coat the chicken.
3. Heat oil in a nonstick 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
4. Reduce the heat to medium-low and continue cooking the chicken until juices run clear when pierced with a knife (about 15 minutes longer). Turn the chicken occasionally.
5. Remove the chicken to a platter.
6. In a 2-cup measuring cup, mix together flour, bouillon, and boiling water until smooth.
7. Stir this mixture into the skillet over medium-high heat until it boils and thickens for 1 minute.
8. Remove from the heat and stir in 1/4 cup buttermilk.
9. Serve the gravy with the chicken. Garnish the platter with fresh thyme sprigs.
Nutritional info per serving: 280 calories, 10g fat, 116mg cholesterol, 845mg sodium.
